---
title: "Models"
author: "Doris Mbitazi Asongafac"
date: "2025-07-14"
output: html_document
---


```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(randomForest)
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(xgboost)
library(pROC) 
library(corrplot)
library(dplyr)
library(car)
```


```{r}
# Load the final dataset
df <- read.csv("final_data_clean.csv")

#Check missing values
sum(is.na(df))

#Structure of the data
str(df)
```

```{r}
# Convert incorrect data types to correct data types
df$pfoa_detected <- as.factor(df$pfoa_detected)
df$state <- as.factor(df$state)
df$water_use <- as.factor(df$water_use)
df$tritium_category <- as.factor(df$tritium_category)
df$month <- as.factor(df$month)
df$median_income <- as.numeric(df$median_income)

# Filter states with at less than 5 observations
state_counts <- df %>% 
  count(state) %>% 
  filter(n >= 5)

df <- df %>% 
  filter(state %in% state_counts$state)

cat("States included:", paste(sort(state_counts$state), collapse = ", "), "\n\n")

# Double-check structure
str(df)

```

```{r}
#Inspecting the raw data (already done with str(df))
# Also use:
summary(df) 
skimr::skim(df) 

```

```{r}
# Select only numeric variables for correlation matrix
numeric_vars <- names(df)[sapply(df, is.numeric)]

print("Numeric variables identified:")
print(numeric_vars)

# Create correlation matrix for numeric variables
# Using base R method for compatibility
correlation_data <- df[, numeric_vars]
correlation_data <- correlation_data[complete.cases(correlation_data), ]

# Calculate correlation matrix
cor_matrix <- cor(correlation_data, use = "complete.obs")

print("Correlation matrix dimensions:")
print(dim(cor_matrix))
```

```{r}
corrplot(cor_matrix, 
         method = "color",
         type = "upper",
         order = "hclust",
         addCoef.col = "black",
         number.cex = 0.6,
         tl.cex = 0.8,
         tl.col = "black",
         tl.srt = 45,
         title = "Correlation Matrix with Values",
         mar = c(0,0,2,0))

```


```{r}
# Find highly correlated pairs (absolute correlation > 0.7)
high_cor_threshold <- 0.7

# Get upper triangle of correlation matrix
upper_tri <- upper.tri(cor_matrix)
cor_matrix_upper <- cor_matrix
cor_matrix_upper[!upper_tri] <- NA

# Convert to data frame and find high correlations
high_correlations <- which(abs(cor_matrix_upper) > high_cor_threshold, arr.ind = TRUE)

if(nrow(high_correlations) > 0) {
  high_cor_pairs <- data.frame(
    Variable1 = rownames(cor_matrix)[high_correlations[,1]],
    Variable2 = colnames(cor_matrix)[high_correlations[,2]],
    Correlation = cor_matrix_upper[high_correlations]
  ) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(Correlation)))
  
  print(high_cor_pairs)
} else {
  print(paste("No variable pairs found with |correlation| >", high_cor_threshold))
}

```



```{r}
#Log transformation of continuous response variable
#Because its highly right skewed
df$log_pfoa <- log(df$pfoa_val + 1)

#Verify the change
str(df)
```

#Linear regression model
```{r}
model <- lm(log_pfoa ~ .-longitude-latitude-median_income-pfoa_detected-pfoa_val-poverty_rate-pct_older-pct_hispanic-unemployment_rate-tritium_category, data=df)
stepwise=step(model,  direction='both')
summary(stepwise)
```


```{r}
vif(stepwise)
```


```{r}
model2 = lm(log_pfoa ~ state + tritium_tu + month + pct_white, data = df)
summary(model2)
```


```{r}
vif(model2)
```
#XGBoost Model
```{r}
library(xgboost)
library(caret)

# Select key predictors based on Random Forest importance and domain knowledge
selected_vars <- c("well_depth", "tritium_tu", "month", "pct_adults", 
                   "pct_older", "water_use", "pct_children", "cancer_rate",
                   "pop_density_per_sq_mile", "pct_bachelor_plus", "state",
                   "median_income", "pct_black", "pct_white", "pct_hispanic",
                   "unemployment_rate", "poverty_rate")

# Create modeling dataset
model_data <- df[, c(selected_vars, "pfoa_detected")]

# Convert factors to numeric for XGBoost
model_data$month <- as.numeric(model_data$month)
model_data$water_use <- as.numeric(model_data$water_use)
model_data$state <- as.numeric(model_data$state)
model_data$pfoa_detected <- as.numeric(model_data$pfoa_detected) - 1  # Convert to 0/1

# Split data
set.seed(123)
train_index <- createDataPartition(model_data$pfoa_detected, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- model_data[train_index, ]
test_data <- model_data[-train_index, ]

# Prepare matrices for XGBoost
train_x <- as.matrix(train_data[, -ncol(train_data)])
train_y <- train_data$pfoa_detected
test_x <- as.matrix(test_data[, -ncol(test_data)])
test_y <- test_data$pfoa_detected

# Train XGBoost model
xgb_model <- xgboost(
  data = train_x,
  label = train_y,
  objective = "binary:logistic",
  nrounds = 100,
  max_depth = 6,
  eta = 0.1,
  subsample = 0.8,
  colsample_bytree = 0.8,
  verbose = 0
)

# Get feature importance
importance_matrix <- xgb.importance(colnames(train_x), model = xgb_model)
print(importance_matrix)

# Plot feature importance
xgb.plot.importance(importance_matrix, top_n = 15)

# Make predictions and evaluate
predictions <- predict(xgb_model, test_x)
pred_binary <- ifelse(predictions > 0.5, 1, 0)

# Confusion matrix
conf_matrix <- table(Predicted = pred_binary, Actual = test_y)
print(conf_matrix)

# Accuracy
accuracy <- sum(diag(conf_matrix)) / sum(conf_matrix)
cat("Accuracy:", round(accuracy, 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
library(xgboost)
library(caret)
library(pROC)

# [Your existing data preparation code...]

# Make predictions (probabilities)
predictions_prob <- predict(xgb_model, test_x)
pred_binary <- ifelse(predictions_prob > 0.5, 1, 0)

# Detailed Performance Metrics
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(as.factor(pred_binary), as.factor(test_y), positive = "1")
print(conf_matrix)

# Manual calculation if needed
sensitivity <- conf_matrix$table[2,2] / (conf_matrix$table[2,2] + conf_matrix$table[1,2])
specificity <- conf_matrix$table[1,1] / (conf_matrix$table[1,1] + conf_matrix$table[2,1])
f1_score <- 2 * (conf_matrix$byClass["Pos Pred Value"] * sensitivity) / 
            (conf_matrix$byClass["Pos Pred Value"] + sensitivity)

cat("Sensitivity:", round(sensitivity, 4), "\n")
cat("Specificity:", round(specificity, 4), "\n")
cat("F1-Score:", round(f1_score, 4), "\n")

# AUC-ROC
roc_curve <- roc(test_y, predictions_prob)
auc_score <- auc(roc_curve)
cat("AUC:", round(auc_score, 4), "\n")
```
```{r}
# SHAP values for direction interpretation
library(SHAPforxgboost)

# Calculate SHAP values
shap_values <- shap.values(xgb_model = xgb_model, X_train = train_x)
shap_long <- shap.prep(xgb_model = xgb_model, X_train = train_x)

# Summary plot showing positive/negative effects
shap.plot.summary(shap_long)

# Individual variable effects
shap.plot.dependence(data_long = shap_long, x = "tritium_tu", y = "tritium_tu", color_feature = "well_depth")
```

```{r}
library(caret)
library(pROC)

# Select key predictors (same as before)
selected_vars <- c("well_depth", "tritium_tu", "month", "pct_adults", 
                   "pct_older", "water_use", "pct_children", "cancer_rate",
                   "pop_density_per_sq_mile", "pct_bachelor_plus", "state",
                   "median_income", "pct_black", "pct_white", "pct_hispanic",
                   "unemployment_rate", "poverty_rate")

# Create modeling dataset
model_data <- df[, c(selected_vars, "pfoa_detected")]

# Split data
set.seed(123)
train_index <- createDataPartition(model_data$pfoa_detected, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- model_data[train_index, ]
test_data <- model_data[-train_index, ]

# Fit logistic regression model
logit_model <- glm(pfoa_detected ~ ., 
                   data = train_data, 
                   family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# Model summary with coefficients and p-values
summary(logit_model)

# Make predictions
predictions_prob <- predict(logit_model, test_data, type = "response")
pred_binary <- ifelse(predictions_prob > 0.5, 1, 0)

# Detailed Performance Metrics
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(as.factor(pred_binary), 
                              as.factor(test_data$pfoa_detected), 
                              positive = "1")
print(conf_matrix)

# AUC-ROC
roc_curve <- roc(test_data$pfoa_detected, predictions_prob)
auc_score <- auc(roc_curve)
cat("AUC:", round(auc_score, 4), "\n")

# Plot ROC curve
plot(roc_curve, main = "ROC Curve for Logistic Regression")

# Extract significant variables (p < 0.05)
coefficients_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(coef(logit_model)),
  Coefficient = coef(logit_model),
  P_value = summary(logit_model)$coefficients[,4],
  Significant = summary(logit_model)$coefficients[,4] < 0.05
)

# Show significant predictors
significant_vars <- coefficients_df[coefficients_df$Significant == TRUE, ]
print("Significant Predictors:")
print(significant_vars)

# Odds ratios for interpretation
odds_ratios <- exp(coef(logit_model))
print("Odds Ratios:")
print(odds_ratios)
```

```{r}
library(caret)
library(pROC)

# Select key predictors (same as before)
selected_vars <- c("well_depth", "tritium_tu", "month", "pct_adults", 
                   "pct_older", "water_use", "pct_children", "cancer_rate",
                   "pop_density_per_sq_mile", "pct_bachelor_plus", "state",
                   "median_income", "pct_black", "pct_white", "pct_hispanic",
                   "unemployment_rate", "poverty_rate")

# Create modeling dataset
model_data <- df[, c(selected_vars, "pfoa_detected")]

# Check for separation issues
table(model_data$pfoa_detected, model_data$state)  # Check state distribution
table(model_data$pfoa_detected, model_data$month)  # Check month distribution
table(model_data$pfoa_detected, model_data$water_use)  # Check water_use distribution

# Remove problematic variables or combine levels
# Option 1: Remove state (likely causing separation)
model_data_fixed <- model_data[, !names(model_data) %in% c("state")]

# Option 2: Use regularized logistic regression instead
library(glmnet)

# Split data
set.seed(123)
train_index <- createDataPartition(model_data_fixed$pfoa_detected, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- model_data_fixed[train_index, ]
test_data <- model_data_fixed[-train_index, ]

# Prepare matrix for glmnet (handles factors automatically)
train_x <- model.matrix(pfoa_detected ~ ., data = train_data)[,-1]  # Remove intercept
train_y <- as.numeric(train_data$pfoa_detected) - 1  # Convert to 0/1
test_x <- model.matrix(pfoa_detected ~ ., data = test_data)[,-1]
test_y <- as.numeric(test_data$pfoa_detected) - 1

# Fit regularized logistic regression (Ridge/Lasso)
cv_model <- cv.glmnet(train_x, train_y, family = "binomial", alpha = 0.5)  # Elastic net
best_lambda <- cv_model$lambda.min

# Final model
logit_model <- glmnet(train_x, train_y, family = "binomial", 
                     alpha = 0.5, lambda = best_lambda)

# Coefficients
coefficients <- coef(logit_model)
print("Model Coefficients:")
print(coefficients)

# Make predictions
predictions_prob <- predict(logit_model, test_x, type = "response")
pred_binary <- ifelse(predictions_prob > 0.5, 1, 0)

# Performance metrics
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(as.factor(pred_binary), 
                              as.factor(test_y), 
                              positive = "1")
print(conf_matrix)

# AUC
roc_curve <- roc(test_y, as.numeric(predictions_prob))
auc_score <- auc(roc_curve)
cat("AUC:", round(auc_score, 4), "\n")
```


```{r}
# Check the distribution of the response variable
table(df$pfoa_detected)
```


```{r}
# Split data into training and testing sets (70/30 split)
train_index <- createDataPartition(df$pfoa_detected, p = 0.7, list = FALSE)
train_data <- df[train_index, ]
test_data <- df[-train_index, ]

# Prepare predictor variables (exclude pfoa_detected and pfoa_val )
all_vars <- names(df)
exclude_vars <- c("pfoa_detected", "pfoa_val", "log_pfoa", "longitude", "latitude", "tritium_category")
predictors <- setdiff(all_vars, exclude_vars)
print(predictors)

# Check the distribution of the response variable
print(table(df$pfoa_detected))
```


```{r}
# RANDOM FOREST MODEL
rf <- as.formula(paste("pfoa_detected ~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")))

# Train Random Forest model
rf_model <- randomForest(
  rf,
  data = train_data,
  ntree = 500,
  mtry = floor(sqrt(length(predictors))),
  importance = TRUE,
  na.action = na.omit
)

# Print Random Forest results
print(rf_model)
```

```{r}
# Make predictions on test set
rf_predictions <- predict(rf_model, test_data)
rf_confusion <- confusionMatrix(rf_predictions, test_data$pfoa_detected)
print(rf_confusion)

```

```{r}
# Variable Importance Plot

importance_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = rownames(importance(rf_model)),
  MeanDecreaseAccuracy = importance(rf_model)[, "MeanDecreaseAccuracy"],
  MeanDecreaseGini = importance(rf_model)[, "MeanDecreaseGini"]
)

# Create importance plot
importance_plot <- ggplot(importance_df, aes(x = reorder(Variable, MeanDecreaseAccuracy), 
                                           y = MeanDecreaseAccuracy)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Random Forest Variable Importance",
       subtitle = "Based on Mean Decrease in Accuracy",
       x = "Variables",
       y = "Mean Decrease in Accuracy") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

print(importance_plot)
```

```{r}
# Print top 10 most important variables
top_vars <- importance_df[order(importance_df$MeanDecreaseAccuracy, decreasing = TRUE), ][1:10, ]
print(top_vars)
```
#balanced data
```{r}
library(ROSE)
balanced_data <- ROSE(pfoa_detected ~ ., data = df, seed = 123)$data
table(df$pfoa_detected)               # Before
table(balanced_data$pfoa_detected)    # After

# Split data into training and testing sets (70/30 split)
train_index <- createDataPartition(balanced_data$pfoa_detected, p = 0.7, list = FALSE)
train_data <- balanced_data[train_index, ]
test_data <- balanced_data[-train_index, ]

# Prepare predictor variables (exclude pfoa_detected and pfoa_val )
all_vars <- names(df)
exclude_vars <- c("pfoa_detected", "pfoa_val", "log_pfoa", "longitude", "latitude", "tritium_category")
predictors <- setdiff(all_vars, exclude_vars)
print(predictors)

```


```{r}
rf <- as.formula(paste("pfoa_detected ~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")))

# Train Random Forest model
r_model <- randomForest(
  rf,
  data = train_data,
  ntree = 500,
  mtry = floor(sqrt(length(predictors))),
  importance = TRUE,
  na.action = na.omit
)

# Print Random Forest results
print(r_model)

# Make predictions on test set
r_predictions <- predict(r_model, test_data)
confusion <- confusionMatrix(r_predictions, test_data$pfoa_detected)
print(confusion)
```

```{r}
# Variable Importance Plot

importance_d <- data.frame(
  Variable = rownames(importance(r_model)),
  MeanDecreaseAccuracy = importance(r_model)[, "MeanDecreaseAccuracy"],
  MeanDecreaseGini = importance(r_model)[, "MeanDecreaseGini"]
)

# Create importance plot
importance <- ggplot(importance_df, aes(x = reorder(Variable, MeanDecreaseAccuracy), 
                                           y = MeanDecreaseAccuracy)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Random Forest Variable Importance",
       subtitle = "Based on Mean Decrease in Accuracy",
       x = "Variables",
       y = "Mean Decrease in Accuracy") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

print(importance)
```

```{r}
# Print top 10 most important variables
top <- importance_d[order(importance_d$MeanDecreaseAccuracy, decreasing = TRUE), ][1:10, ]
print(top)
```


#AutoML for log_pfoa (Regression Task)
```{r}
# Load the h2o library
library(h2o)

# Start H2O 
h2o.init()

# Convert your dataframe to an H2OFrame
df_h2o <- as.h2o(df)

# Define response and predictors - REMOVING specified variables (using log_pfoa as response)
variables_to_remove <- c("latitude", "longitude", "tritium_category", "pfoa_val", "pfoa_detected", "log_pfoa")
x_reg <- setdiff(names(df), variables_to_remove)  # Remove specified vars AND response from predictors
y_reg <- "log_pfoa"

print(paste("Predictors used:", paste(x_reg, collapse = ", ")))
print(paste("Number of predictors:", length(x_reg)))

# Split the data
splits_reg <- h2o.splitFrame(data = df_h2o, ratios = 0.7, seed = 123)
train_reg <- splits_reg[[1]]
test_reg  <- splits_reg[[2]]

# Check response variable distribution
print("Response variable (log_pfoa) summary in training set:")
print(h2o.summary(train_reg[, y_reg]))

# Run AutoML 
aml_reg <- h2o.automl(x = x_reg,
                      y = y_reg,
                      training_frame = train_reg,
                      leaderboard_frame = test_reg,
                      max_models = 20,
                      seed = 1234)

# Show leaderboard with all columns
lb_reg <- h2o.get_leaderboard(aml_reg, extra_columns = "ALL")
print(lb_reg, n = nrow(lb_reg))

```


```{r}
# Best regression model
best_model_reg <- aml_reg@leader
print(best_model_reg)

```


```{r}
# Get model performance on test set
perf_test <- h2o.performance(best_model_reg, test_reg)
print(perf_test)

# Make predictions on test set
predictions_reg <- h2o.predict(best_model_reg, test_reg)
print(head(predictions_reg))

```


```{r}
# Create a comparison of actual vs predicted values
actual_vs_predicted <- h2o.cbind(test_reg[, y_reg], predictions_reg)
colnames(actual_vs_predicted) <- c("actual_log_pfoa", "predicted_log_pfoa")
print(head(actual_vs_predicted, 10))


# Variable importance from the best model
var_imp <- h2o.varimp(best_model_reg)
print(head(var_imp, 10))

```


#AutoML for pfoa_detected (Binary Classification)
```{r}
# Load the h2o library
library(h2o)

# Start H2O 
h2o.init()

# Convert your dataframe to an H2OFrame
df_h2o <- as.h2o(df)

# Convert to factor (in the H2OFrame)
df_h2o[, "pfoa_detected"] <- as.factor(df_h2o[, "pfoa_detected"])

# Define response and predictors - REMOVING specified variables
variables_to_remove <- c("latitude", "longitude", "tritium_category", "log_pfoa", "pfoa_val", "pfoa_detected")
x_clf <- setdiff(names(df), variables_to_remove)
y_clf <- "pfoa_detected"

print(paste("Predictors used:", paste(x_clf, collapse = ", ")))
print(paste("Number of predictors:", length(x_clf)))
```


```{r}
# Split the data
splits_clf <- h2o.splitFrame(data = df_h2o, ratios = 0.7, seed = 123)
train_clf <- splits_clf[[1]]
test_clf  <- splits_clf[[2]]

# Check class distribution before balancing
print(h2o.table(train_clf[, y_clf]))
```


```{r}
# Run AutoML (Classification) with class balancing
aml_clf <- h2o.automl(x = x_clf,
                      y = y_clf,
                      training_frame = train_clf,
                      leaderboard_frame = test_clf,
                      max_models = 20,
                      seed = 1234,
                      balance_classes = TRUE,  # Enable class balancing
                      max_after_balance_size = 3.0)  

# Show leaderboard with all columns
lb_clf <- h2o.get_leaderboard(aml_clf, extra_columns = "ALL")
print(lb_clf, n = nrow(lb_clf))
```


```{r}
# Best classification model
best_model_clf <- aml_clf@leader
print(best_model_clf)
```


```{r}
# Get model performance on test set
perf_test <- h2o.performance(best_model_clf, test_clf)
print(perf_test)

```


```{r}
# Generate confusion matrix
cm <- h2o.confusionMatrix(perf_test)
print(cm)

```


```{r}
# Make predictions on test set
predictions_clf <- h2o.predict(best_model_clf, test_clf)
print(head(predictions_clf))

# Variable importance from the best model
print("Variable Importance (Top 10):")
var_imp <- h2o.varimp(best_model_clf)
print(head(var_imp, 10))
```


```{r}
# Create a proper confusion matrix using R's table function
# Convert actual and predicted values to vectors
actual <- as.vector(test_clf[, "pfoa_detected"])
predicted <- as.vector(h2o.predict(best_model_clf, test_clf)$predict)

# Convert to factors with the same levels
actual <- factor(as.character(actual))
predicted <- factor(as.character(predicted), levels = levels(actual))

# Create confusion matrix
print("Confusion Matrix:")
conf_matrix <- table(Predicted = predicted, Actual = actual)
print(conf_matrix)

# Calculate metrics manually
TP <- conf_matrix["1", "1"]
TN <- conf_matrix["0", "0"]
FP <- conf_matrix["1", "0"]
FN <- conf_matrix["0", "1"]

# Calculate all metrics
total <- sum(conf_matrix)
accuracy <- (TP + TN) / total
sensitivity <- TP / (TP + FN)   # Recall or True Positive Rate
specificity <- TN / (TN + FP)   # True Negative Rate
precision <- TP / (TP + FP)     # Positive Predictive Value
npv <- TN / (TN + FN)          # Negative Predictive Value
f1_score <- 2 * (precision * sensitivity) / (precision + sensitivity)
error_rate <- 1 - accuracy
false_positive_rate <- FP / (FP + TN)
false_negative_rate <- FN / (TP + FN)

# Get AUC from H2O
perf_test <- h2o.performance(best_model_clf, test_clf)
auc_value <- h2o.auc(perf_test)

# Print detailed metrics
cat("\n=== DETAILED CLASSIFICATION METRICS ===\n")
cat("True Positives (TP) :", TP, "\n")
cat("True Negatives (TN) :", TN, "\n")
cat("False Positives (FP):", FP, "\n")
cat("False Negatives (FN):", FN, "\n")
cat("Total Samples       :", total, "\n")

cat("\n=== PERFORMANCE METRICS ===\n")
cat("Accuracy            :", round(accuracy, 4), "\n")
cat("Sensitivity (Recall):", round(sensitivity, 4), "\n")
cat("Specificity         :", round(specificity, 4), "\n")
cat("Precision (PPV)     :", round(precision, 4), "\n")
cat("Negative Pred Value :", round(npv, 4), "\n")
cat("F1 Score            :", round(f1_score, 4), "\n")
cat("Error Rate          :", round(error_rate, 4), "\n")
cat("False Positive Rate :", round(false_positive_rate, 4), "\n")
cat("False Negative Rate :", round(false_negative_rate, 4), "\n")
cat("AUC                 :", round(auc_value, 4), "\n")


```


```{r}
# Summary table
metrics_df <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Accuracy", "Sensitivity", "Specificity", "Precision", 
             "NPV", "F1-Score", "Error Rate", "AUC"),
  Value = c(round(accuracy, 4), round(sensitivity, 4), round(specificity, 4), 
            round(precision, 4), round(npv, 4), round(f1_score, 4), 
            round(error_rate, 4), round(auc_value, 4))
)
print(metrics_df)
```
